sourceMovie.title: Ice Age
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ice Age works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Croods
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ice Age clicked for you, The Croods (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: An American Tail
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ice Age clicked for you, An American Tail (1986) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ice Age?
faq.answer: Try The Croods, An American Tail, Winnie the Pooh—each shares DNA with Ice Age while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ice Age?
faq.answer: Ice Age (2002) is best described as Animation, Comedy, Family, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ice Age part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ice Age Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
